results.rendered_html:
pra-√lubh P. Ā. -lubhyati, ˚te, (Ā.) to lust after, be lustful, follow one's lusts, go astray sexually (said of a wife),  ŚāṅkhGṛ. ;  Mn. ;
to allure, entice, seduce, pollute,  MBh.  :
Caus. -lobhayati, to cause to lust after, allure, entice, attempt, to seduce,  MBh. ;  R. ;  Pur.  &c.;
to divert the attention of any one by (instr.),  Suśr.

results.rendered_html:
pralubh 4 P.
1 To be greedy or desirous, be lustful.
2 To allure, seduce, entice.
3 To pollute (through lust).
— Caus. To allure, attract, entice, seduce.
